title: National Digital Commerce Policy 2018
description: Bangladesh's National Digital Commerce Policy 2018 establishes the strategic framework for the development of e-commerce and digital trade in Bangladesh, setting out the government's vision, goals, and policy interventions to create a thriving digital commerce ecosystem. The Policy addresses legal frameworks, payment systems, logistics, consumer protection, and the development of local e-commerce platforms.

provisions.title: Chapter 1. Vision and Objectives
provisions.content: Bangladesh's National Digital Commerce Policy 2018 envisions a Bangladesh where digital commerce is a significant driver of economic growth, job creation, and poverty reduction, enabling businesses of all sizes and in all parts of the country to reach national and global markets. The Policy's objectives are: (a) to create a conducive legal, regulatory, and infrastructure environment for digital commerce; (b) to protect and promote the rights of consumers in digital commerce transactions; (c) to promote the participation of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (MSMEs) in digital commerce; (d) to develop Bangladesh's domestic digital commerce industry and reduce dependence on foreign platforms; (e) to position Bangladesh as a hub for digital commerce in South Asia; (f) to enable Bangladeshi craftspeople, artisans, and farmers to access markets through digital commerce. Digital commerce shall be developed in a manner that promotes gender equity and inclusion.

provisions.title: Chapter 2. Legal and Regulatory Framework
provisions.content: The Government shall establish a comprehensive legal and regulatory framework for digital commerce, including: (a) recognition of the legal validity of electronic contracts, electronic signatures, and electronic documents; (b) clear rules on consumer protection in digital commerce, including rights of return and refund; (c) a data protection framework ensuring the privacy of consumer data; (d) rules on the liability of digital commerce platforms for products and services sold through them; (e) regulations for digital payment services, including mobile financial services and e-wallets; (f) anti-spam and unsolicited commercial communications rules; (g) rules on the use of algorithms and automated decision-making in digital commerce. The Ministry of Commerce shall lead the development of digital commerce legislation, in coordination with relevant ministries and regulatory bodies. Existing laws shall be reviewed and updated as necessary to ensure they are applicable to digital commerce.

provisions.title: Chapter 3. Digital Payment Infrastructure
provisions.content: The development of robust and affordable digital payment infrastructure is a prerequisite for the growth of digital commerce in Bangladesh. The Policy's approach to digital payments includes: (a) expanding the interoperability of mobile financial services (MFS) systems to enable transfers between different MFS providers; (b) establishing a national payment switch (BangladeshQR) enabling merchants to accept payments from all payment platforms through a single QR code; (c) developing digital payment solutions suitable for low-value microtransactions in the informal economy; (d) promoting the acceptance of digital payments by merchants throughout the country, including through incentive programs; (e) ensuring that digital payment services are accessible and affordable for low-income consumers; (f) implementing robust consumer protection measures in digital payment services. Bangladesh Bank shall have regulatory oversight of all digital payment services and shall promote competition, interoperability, and consumer protection in the sector.

provisions.title: Chapter 4. E-Commerce Logistics
provisions.content: Reliable and affordable logistics services are essential for the growth of e-commerce. The Policy's approach to e-commerce logistics includes: (a) developing a national last-mile delivery network capable of reaching all parts of Bangladesh; (b) promoting the adoption of digital tools for logistics management by courier and delivery companies; (c) simplifying customs procedures for cross-border e-commerce shipments; (d) developing bonded warehouse facilities for e-commerce importers and exporters; (e) promoting the growth of third-party logistics (3PL) services specialized in e-commerce fulfillment; (f) developing rural postal and logistics infrastructure to enable rural businesses to participate in e-commerce. Bangladesh Post Office shall be modernized to provide e-commerce logistics services as a complement to private sector providers. The Government shall work with industry to develop industry standards for packaging, labeling, and tracking of e-commerce shipments.

provisions.title: Chapter 5. Consumer Protection in Digital Commerce
provisions.content: The Policy recognizes that consumer trust is fundamental to the growth of digital commerce and shall establish a comprehensive consumer protection framework. Key consumer protection measures include: (a) mandatory disclosure of trader identity, product information, and terms and conditions; (b) a minimum fourteen-day right of return for goods purchased online without any requirement to provide a reason; (c) secure and encrypted payment processing; (d) protection against unauthorized use of consumer financial data; (e) clear and accessible dispute resolution mechanisms; (f) prohibition of unfair and deceptive digital marketing practices. The Directorate of National Consumer Rights Protection (DNCRP) shall be strengthened to deal with digital commerce consumer complaints. A National Consumer Information Portal shall provide consumers with information about their rights in digital commerce. Sellers and platforms shall be required to display consumer ratings and reviews prominently.

provisions.title: Chapter 6. Support for MSMEs in Digital Commerce
provisions.content: Micro, Small, and Medium Enterprises (MSMEs) shall be supported to participate in and benefit from digital commerce. Support measures include: (a) training programs for MSME owners and employees in digital marketing, online selling, and digital financial management; (b) subsidized access to e-commerce platforms for rural and small businesses; (c) preferential treatment for domestic MSME sellers on government-supported digital commerce platforms; (d) access to digital credit scoring and digital lending products for e-commerce businesses; (e) aggregation programs to enable individual rural producers to sell collectively on digital platforms; (f) development of sector-specific digital marketplaces for key industries. The Bangladesh Export Processing Zones Authority and the SME Foundation shall be involved in implementing MSME support programs. Special programs shall be developed for women entrepreneurs and artisans selling traditional products online.

provisions.title: Chapter 7. Cross-Border Digital Commerce
provisions.content: Bangladesh shall promote cross-border digital commerce as a driver of export growth and foreign exchange earnings. Policy measures for cross-border digital commerce include: (a) allowing Bangladeshi businesses to receive international payments through digital channels without the need to open a physical bank account; (b) simplifying customs clearance for export shipments from digital commerce; (c) developing e-commerce pavilions in export processing zones and economic zones; (d) negotiating digital trade provisions in bilateral and regional trade agreements; (e) promoting Bangladeshi sellers on international e-commerce platforms including Amazon, Alibaba, and regional platforms. Bangladesh Bank shall revise foreign exchange regulations to enable digital cross-border payments for e-commerce. The National Board of Revenue shall simplify export procedures for small and micro-exporters using digital commerce platforms. Bangladesh shall pursue digital trade agreements with partner countries.

provisions.title: Chapter 8. Implementation and Governance
provisions.content: The implementation of the National Digital Commerce Policy shall be coordinated by the Ministry of Commerce, with support from the Ministry of Posts, Telecommunications and Information Technology and other relevant agencies. A National Digital Commerce Development Committee shall be established with representatives from government, industry, and consumer organizations to oversee implementation. Annual implementation plans shall be developed with specific targets, timelines, and budgets. Progress shall be measured against a set of key performance indicators to be specified in the implementation plan. The digital commerce sector shall be supported through a Digital Commerce Development Fund financed through the national budget and contributions from industry. International development partners and investors shall be invited to support the development of digital commerce infrastructure and capacity. An annual Digital Commerce Expo shall be organized to showcase Bangladesh's digital commerce ecosystem and promote international investment.

definitions.definition: Any commercial transaction or activity conducted wholly or partly by electronic means, including e-commerce, m-commerce, digital financial services, and online marketplaces.
Financial services including mobile banking, mobile payments, and mobile wallets delivered through mobile phones and related technologies, regulated by Bangladesh Bank.
Outsourced logistics and fulfillment services provided by specialized companies to e-commerce businesses, including warehousing, order processing, packaging, and delivery.
